name: Payments (Webhook)
description:
Documentation-only folder for the Stripe webhook receiver. **You cannot meaningfully test this from Postman directly** because the request must be signed by Stripe with our `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Postman doesn't sign payloads with HMAC-SHA256 the way Stripe does.

For real testing, use the Stripe CLI on your dev machine:

```bash
stripe listen --forward-to http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/webhook
```

Full walkthrough (signing secret extraction, replay, test cards, overbook simulation) lives in `docs/runbooks/stripe-testing.md`.

The request below sends an unsigned payload so you can verify the **rejection path** end-to-end — the response should be `400 STRIPE_WEBHOOK_INVALID`. A successful 200 happens only via the Stripe CLI flow.

request.description:
Posts a Stripe-shaped event WITHOUT a valid `Stripe-Signature` header. The backend must reject with 400 `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_INVALID`. This proves the signature-verification gate is wired correctly — if you see 200, the gate is bypassed and the runbook setup is wrong.

**Note:** the response is plain `{ data: null, error: ... }` because the global filter runs even when the controller opts out of `TransformInterceptor`. Only successful 2xx responses skip the envelope.
